title: Asuka is out of his league
display_title: Asuka is out of his league
wikitext:
[]: Asuka is "out of his league"             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Dec 25, 2010 3:44 pm (Split from here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=410114#410114 ) Mr. Tines wrote: More that she's out of his league

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 25, 2010 3:58 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Superficial reason enough?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:02 pm Kendrix wrote: I demand proof.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:07 pm Bagheera wrote: because Shinji's pretty popular in his own right and because Asuka's damaged enough (and abrasive enough) that she's not as hot as she's made out to be.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:10 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Still doesn't address the key issue -- social class. She's an aristocrat, another warrior-noble out of a respectable German pedigree on the one side, he's barely middle class. They come from different worlds.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:15 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Still doesn't address the key issue -- social class. She's an aristocrat, another warrior-noble out of a respectable German pedigree on the one side, he's barely middle class. They come from different worlds.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:29 pm While the Japanese might be looking down at this round eyed devil, that doesn't stop her looking down at them in return -- recall all her disparaging culture-shock remarks after her arrival. You just have to look at her general demeanour before she gets roflstomped by circumstance -- hauty disdain for pretty much everything outside the circle of the elect to which she has belonged from her earliest memories. As for her family -- severing connections with a father (an arriviste by name and nationality) who clearly meant far less to her than her mother did does not mean losing connection with the pedigree on her mother's side.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:32 pm Mr. Tines wrote: While the Japanese might be looking down at this round eyed devil, that doesn't stop her looking down at them in return -- recall all her disparaging culture-shock remarks after her arrival. You just have to look at her general demeanour before she gets roflstomped by circumstance -- hauty disdain for pretty much everything outside the circle of the elect to which she has belonged from her earliest memories. As for her family -- severing connections with a father (an arriviste by name and nationality) who clearly meant far less to her than her mother did does not mean losing connection with the pedigree on her mother's side.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:34 pm Bagheera wrote: (and it apparently didn't stop her father from marrying some riff-raff he met while visiting his wife in the hospital...).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:47 pm Mr. Tines wrote: As for her family -- severing connections with a father (an arriviste by name and nationality) who clearly meant far less to her than her mother did does not mean losing connection with the pedigree on her mother's side.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 25, 2010 4:50 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Miyamura herself said she was directed to play Shikinami as a different character from Soryu, our very own Fazmotron was there: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=9635

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:14 pm wait...Asuka's nobility? what? huh? I'm lost...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:27 pm tehprognoob wrote: wait...Asuka's nobility? what? huh? I'm lost...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:28 pm What confuses me more than anything is Tines's seeming presupposition that modern German has aristocracy in any meaningful way

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:29 pm WHAT??? I'm MORE confused...is Langley a name tied to medieval nobility, then? Or is Soryu? When did that start? Explain from the beginning please?

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:38 pm It's all very simple. Mr. Tines said that Asuka was out of Shinji's league, and that premise has gotten less and less sustainable as the discussion has gone on. He is now clutching at straws. Everything more complex than that is just window dressing.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:44 pm tehprognoob wrote: WHAT??? I'm MORE confused...is Langley a name tied to medieval nobility, then? Or is Soryu? When did that start? Explain from the beginning please?

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:50 pm tehprognoob wrote: WHAT??? I'm MORE confused...is Langley a name tied to medieval nobility, then? Or is Soryu? When did that start? Explain from the beginning please?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:56 pm Bagheera wrote: Asuka's mother is Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu. Zeppelin (presumably her mother's name) is the name of a German aristocratic family dating back to, what, 1400 or so. The line came to prominence in the 19th century, and really took off with the famous airships around the turn of the 20th century. AFAIK the family still uses titles (or did recently; a countess got into it with the band Led Zeppelin over its use of her family name), but I don't know how that works when you marry into a foreign family and relocate (as Kyoko's mother apparently did). When you further dilute the blood, such that your progeny is only 1/4 German (as in Asuka's case), all pretense of noble standing is pretty much gone. And that's assuming the name meant fuck-all in the here and now anyway, which is debatable.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:59 pm Kendrix wrote: This barely means that Anno didn't give her any answer on that matter.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 25, 2010 3:58 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Superficial reason enough?
